mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near zero lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically inflating present value of expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains below its trailing 12-month average.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS is below its trailing average (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that leads equity risk premia to widen, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following a >0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over the same 3-month window reflects a broad consumer and investment demand contraction that is not captured by either series alone, and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days) because earnings expectations lag the demand slowdown.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change < 0 AND HOUST 3-month change < 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns conditional on joint RSAFS/HOUST decline are not significantly more negative than unconditional returns (p>0.05), or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal in Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a deceleration in industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals peaking capacity utilization and margin compression ahead of a demand-supply mismatch, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship is weak or absent in early-cycle recoveries.
failure_condition: The negative correlation between INDPRO deceleration and forward returns fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) when restricted to periods independently classified as late-cycle expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption capacity, producing modest positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as the market incorporates incremental labor strength.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant difference between periods of positive and negative PAYEMS month-over-month growth (t-test p>0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate crosses above 2.5%, tightening financial conditions begin to materially raise corporate borrowing costs and compress valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days until the rate falls back below that threshold.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS crossing above 2.5% are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 in the tightening subsample.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent downtrend in housing starts (HOUST) over 6 months reflects weakening forward-looking construction demand and household investment appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days) as the effect propagates through consumption and credit channels.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during HOUST downtrends are not significantly lower than during HOUST uptrends (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-like joint condition where UNRATE rises meaningfully while FEDFUNDS remains elevated relative to its own trailing trend indicates the central bank is behind the curve on a labor deterioration, triggering a sharp negative repricing of equities over the next 20 trading days; this interaction is distinct from either series' independent signal and is most powerful entering a recessionary regime.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month rise > 0.3pp AND FEDFUNDS above its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns conditional on the joint UNRATE/FEDFUNDS trigger are not significantly more negative than the UNRATE-only signal's conditional returns (p>0.05), invalidating the interaction premise.
